compiled_state.title: As a non-engineer exploring DSO, I can read a root README that describes what the plugin does in plain language and links me to the installation guide
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: A rewritten root README.md that describes the DSO plugin in plain, accessible language -- explaining what it does, who it is for, and where to go next.
**Why**: The current root README is nearly empty. Non-engineers joining a team that uses DSO have no starting point. The README is the first touchpoint for anyone discovering the repository.
**Scope**:
- IN: Rewrite root README.md for a non-engineer audience; include a link to root INSTALL.md; describe what DSO does in plain language without unexplained jargon
- OUT: Installation steps (INSTALL.md / Story C); configuration details; integration setup; ast-grep content migration (handled by Story C)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, root README.md describes what DSO does in plain language understandable to a non-engineer, with no unexplained acronyms or jargon
  <- Satisfies: "Root README.md describes what DSO does in plain language understandable to a non-engineer"
- When this story is complete, root README.md contains a link to root INSTALL.md
  <- Satisfies: "README contains a link to INSTALL.md"

## Considerations

- [Maintainability] This story depends on Story C because the README links to root INSTALL.md, which Story C creates. Story B must execute after Story C so the link target exists and can be verified.

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue -- one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
